---
title: "Retail Sales Data with Seasonal Trends & Marketing"
subtitle: "Project Proposal ‚Äî Group 1"
author: "Jake Evans ¬∑ Mickyas Shawel ¬∑ Aldo Naranjo ¬∑ Luis Berumen ¬∑ Tim Arana"
institute: "IBM 6540 ‚Äî Applied Machine Learning for Marketing | Cal Poly Pomona"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: slide
    embed-resources: true
    footer: "IBM 6540 ‚Äî Group 1 Project Proposal | Summer 2026"
    toc: false
---

## Group 1

| Member | RO Lead |
|---|---|
| Jake Evans | RO __ |
| Mickyas Shawel | RO __ |
| Aldo Naranjo | RO __ |
| Luis Berumen | RO __ |
| Tim Arana | RO __ |

::: aside
Each member leads one Research Objective (fill in assignments above).
:::

## Data & Repository

-   **Dataset:** Retail Sales Data with Seasonal Trends & Marketing (Kaggle)
    -   <https://www.kaggle.com/datasets/abdullah0a/retail-sales-data-with-seasonal-trends-and-marketing>
    -   30,000 records of transaction-level retail sales
-   **Group Repo:** <https://github.com/mjshawell/IBM-6540-Group-Project>

# Ch1: Introduction {background-color="#23351F"}

## Problem Definition

::: callout-note
## Group: draft together

-   Who is the audience/client? (e.g., a multi-location retail chain's marketing team)
-   What decision are they struggling with? (e.g., when to spend on marketing, how deep to discount, how to plan for seasonal demand)
-   One or two sentences framing the business problem.
:::

## Research Objectives

-   **RO1 ‚Äî Seasonality:** How do sales revenue and units sold vary across seasons and months?
-   **RO2 ‚Äî Marketing:** What is the relationship between marketing spend and sales revenue?
-   **RO3 ‚Äî Discounting:** How does discount percentage affect units sold and revenue?
-   **RO4 ‚Äî Holidays & Timing:** How do holiday periods and day of the week influence sales?
-   **RO5 ‚Äî Category & Location:** How does sales performance differ by product category and store location?

::: aside
*Draft ROs ‚Äî group to revise wording and confirm one lead per RO.*
:::

## Importance of the Topic

::: callout-note
## Group: draft together

Why does this matter for the audience? Starting angles:

-   Seasonal planning affects inventory, staffing, and budgets
-   Marketing dollars need ROI justification
-   Discounts drive volume but can erode margin
-   Findings translate into an actionable promotion calendar
:::

# Ch3: Methods {background-color="#23351F"}

## Population

::: callout-note
## Group: define

The population the sample represents ‚Äî e.g., retail sales transactions across multiple stores, locations, and product categories. Note that this is a synthetic/educational dataset and state what real-world population it stands in for.
:::

## Sampling / Data Collection

-   Secondary data: public Kaggle dataset (link on slide 2)
-   30,000 transaction records
-   **Key variables:**
    -   Sales Revenue (USD), Units Sold
    -   Discount Percentage, Marketing Spend (USD)
    -   Date, Day of the Week, Holiday Effect
    -   Store ID, Store Location, Product Category

## Data Wrangling

-   **From records to data:** import CSV with `readr::read_csv()`; store in repo `data/` folder
-   **Tidying:** clean column names (`janitor::clean_names()`); check missing values and duplicates; convert `Date` to date type; make `Holiday Effect`, `Day of the Week`, `Product Category`, `Store Location` factors
-   **Transforming:** derive `month`, `season`, and `year` from `Date`; create spend/discount tiers as needed per RO

# Research Objectives {background-color="#23351F"}

## RO1: Seasonality ‚Äî Lead: ______

-   **IVs:** month, season (derived from `Date`)
-   **DVs:** Sales Revenue, Units Sold
-   **Wrangling plan:** extract month/season from `Date`; aggregate revenue and units by month
-   **Visualization plan:** line chart of monthly revenue; seasonal boxplots

## RO2: Marketing Spend ‚Äî Lead: ______

-   **IV:** Marketing Spend (USD)
-   **DV:** Sales Revenue
-   **Wrangling plan:** flag zero-spend vs. positive-spend transactions; create spend tiers
-   **Visualization plan:** scatterplot of spend vs. revenue with trend line; tier comparison bars

## RO3: Discounting ‚Äî Lead: ______

-   **IV:** Discount Percentage
-   **DVs:** Units Sold, Sales Revenue
-   **Wrangling plan:** bin discounts (none / low / medium / high); compare distributions across bins
-   **Visualization plan:** boxplots of units sold by discount bin; discount vs. revenue scatter

## RO4: Holidays & Timing ‚Äî Lead: ______

-   **IVs:** Holiday Effect, Day of the Week
-   **DVs:** Sales Revenue, Units Sold
-   **Wrangling plan:** confirm factor levels; order days Mon‚ÄìSun; group and summarize by holiday flag and day
-   **Visualization plan:** grouped bar chart of average revenue by day, split by holiday vs. non-holiday

## RO5: Category & Location ‚Äî Lead: ______

-   **IVs:** Product Category, Store Location
-   **DVs:** Sales Revenue, Units Sold
-   **Wrangling plan:** summarize revenue/units by category and location; identify top and bottom performers
-   **Visualization plan:** faceted bar charts by location; category heatmap

# Conclusion {background-color="#23351F"}

## Conclusion

::: callout-note
## Group: draft together

-   Restate the problem and the five ROs in one or two lines
-   What the client gains: a data-driven view of seasonality, promotion effectiveness, and category/location performance
-   Next steps toward Ch4 (Results) and Ch5 (Recommendations).
:::

## Thank You

**Questions?**

Group 1 ‚Äî IBM 6540 | Summer 2026

Repo: <https://github.com/mjshawell/IBM-6540-Group-Project>
